# pitlaneR

Access Formula 1 data from R using the free [Jolpica
API](https://jolpi.ca) (Ergast-compatible). No API key or Python
dependency required.

Important: the current version of this R package was largely produce by
Claude, as part of Posit’s AI beta. This code is only partially tested,
and so should be treated with caution. Read about this process [on my
blog](https://benjackman.github.io/tinkering/#claude-builds-an-r-package).

## Installation

``` r
# Install from GitHub
# install.packages("pak")
pak::pak("benjackman/pitlaneR")
```

## Quick start

``` r
library(pitlaneR)

# Race results for a specific round
f1_results(2024, 1)

# Full season results
f1_results(2024)

# Current championship standings
f1_driver_standings(2024)
f1_constructor_standings(2024)

# Qualifying results
f1_qualifying(2024, 1)

# Drivers and constructors
f1_drivers(2024)
f1_constructors(2024)

# Race schedule
f1_races(2024)

# Circuit info
f1_circuits()

# Lap times (available from 1996+)
f1_laps(2024, 1)

# Pit stops (available from 2012+)
f1_pit_stops(2024, 1)

# Sprint results
f1_sprint(2024)

# All F1 seasons
f1_seasons()
```

## Features

- **Tidy output**: All functions return tibbles with snake_case column
  names
- **Automatic pagination**: Large result sets are fetched across
  multiple API pages automatically
- **Session caching**: Repeated calls are served from an in-memory cache
  (reset with
  [`f1_clear_cache()`](https://benjackman.github.io/pitlaneR/reference/f1_clear_cache.md))
- **Historical coverage**: Data from 1950 to the present season

## Data source

All data comes from the [Jolpica F1 API](https://jolpi.ca), a free,
community-maintained successor to the Ergast Motor Racing API.

## License

MIT

# Package index

## Race Results

- [`f1_results()`](https://benjackman.github.io/pitlaneR/reference/f1_results.md)
  : Get F1 race results
- [`f1_qualifying()`](https://benjackman.github.io/pitlaneR/reference/f1_qualifying.md)
  : Get F1 qualifying results
- [`f1_sprint()`](https://benjackman.github.io/pitlaneR/reference/f1_sprint.md)
  : Get F1 sprint race results

## Championship Standings

- [`f1_driver_standings()`](https://benjackman.github.io/pitlaneR/reference/f1_driver_standings.md)
  : Get F1 driver championship standings
- [`f1_constructor_standings()`](https://benjackman.github.io/pitlaneR/reference/f1_constructor_standings.md)
  : Get F1 constructor championship standings

## Reference Data

- [`f1_seasons()`](https://benjackman.github.io/pitlaneR/reference/f1_seasons.md)
  : Get all F1 seasons
- [`f1_races()`](https://benjackman.github.io/pitlaneR/reference/f1_races.md)
  : Get F1 race schedule
- [`f1_circuits()`](https://benjackman.github.io/pitlaneR/reference/f1_circuits.md)
  : Get F1 circuit information
- [`f1_drivers()`](https://benjackman.github.io/pitlaneR/reference/f1_drivers.md)
  : Get F1 driver information
- [`f1_constructors()`](https://benjackman.github.io/pitlaneR/reference/f1_constructors.md)
  : Get F1 constructor information

## Detailed Timing

- [`f1_laps()`](https://benjackman.github.io/pitlaneR/reference/f1_laps.md)
  : Get F1 lap time data
- [`f1_pit_stops()`](https://benjackman.github.io/pitlaneR/reference/f1_pit_stops.md)
  : Get F1 pit stop data

## Utilities

- [`f1_clear_cache()`](https://benjackman.github.io/pitlaneR/reference/f1_clear_cache.md)
  : Clear the pitlaneR session cache

### All vignettes

- [Getting Started with
  pitlaneR](https://benjackman.github.io/pitlaneR/articles/pitlaneR.md):
